summary.intent: Fix MiniMax API rejecting system role messages (issue #592) by restoring system prompt extraction that was accidentally reverted in PR #586, then refactor for future-proofing.
summary.outcome: Restored and improved system prompt extraction in openai.rs with a future-proof API (`requires_top_level_system_prompt` + `apply_top_level_system_prompt`), all 8 MiniMax tests and 262 provider tests pass, clippy clean. Commit staged and push/PR creation requested.

repo: MiniMax API rejects `role: "system"` in the messages array with error 2013; system prompts must go in a top-level `system` field.
PR #586 (f96dcc46) accidentally reverted the MiniMax fix from commit 1bf8e9b9 because it was only verified with mocks, not the live API.
The pattern of extracting system prompts is already used in anthropic.rs (`to_anthropic_messages`) and openai_compat.rs (`split_responses_instructions_and_input`).
The `just test` and `just lint` commands fail on this machine due to a pre-existing CUDA/aws-lc-sys build environment issue unrelated to the codebase.

code.finding: Added `requires_top_level_system_prompt()` (detects MiniMax by model prefix, provider name, or base URL) and `apply_top_level_system_prompt(&mut body)` (extracts system messages from body's messages array, joins with \n\n, injects as top-level `system` field) after `requires_reasoning_content_on_tool_messages`.

Three request paths (probe_chat_completions, stream_with_tools_sse, complete) each need `self.apply_top_level_system_prompt(&mut body)` called after body construction to handle system prompt extraction.

workflow: Naming methods by intent (`requires_top_level_system_prompt`) rather than provider (`is_minimax`) makes extension safer and self-documenting.
Combining extraction and injection into a single `apply_*(&mut body)` method prevents the two-step pattern from being partially applied — the exact class of bug that caused the regression.
Clippy caught a collapsible nested `if` that needed fixing; running clippy per-crate (`cargo clippy -p`) is faster than full workspace when only one crate was changed.
Background tasks in Claude Code capture stale state if the code changes between task launch and completion — verify results are from current code.

friction: The `just lint` and `just test` commands fail due to a CUDA Toolkit / aws-lc-sys build environment issue, making workspace-level verification impossible on this machine.
Clippy flagged a collapsible `if` in the new code, requiring an extra fix pass after the refactor.
Issue #592 doesn't exist in the local beads tracker, so issue claiming was skipped.
open_items: Push branch and create PR (commit was staged but transcript ends before push/PR creation completes).
Smoke-test against the live MiniMax API to confirm the fix works end-to-end (was explicitly called out as the check missed in PR #586).
The `just lint` / `just test` environment failure (CUDA/aws-lc-sys) is a pre-existing machine issue that should be investigated separately.
